top_signals.title: Gulf Producers Race to Load Oil and LNG as Hormuz Stays Open
top_signals.summary: Saudi Arabia, the United Arab Emirates, and Qatar continue loading oil and LNG onto vessels at their Persian Gulf ports despite the attacks on two commercial vessels near the Strait of Hormuz and the weekend tensions between the U.S. and Iran. The Middle Eastern producers are rushing to load oil and LNG while the Strait of Hormuz is formally open, even if traffic has slowed since Friday, due to the attack on a fully-laden oil supertanker in the Strait of Hormuz, Reuters reported on Monday, quoting ship-tracking data.   Iran is also observed…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: US Supreme Court blocks Donald Trump from sacking Fed governor Lisa Cook
top_signals.summary: Justices fail to over-rule the president from firing a regulator in a separate decision
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Supreme Court rules Trump cannot fire Fed Governor Lisa Cook for now
top_signals.summary: The ruling by the Supreme Court means that Lisa Cook will remain as a Federal Reserve governor as her lawsuit challenging Trump's effort to fire her proceeds.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: Mexico Exchange Halts Trading Ahead of Session Open
top_signals.summary: Mexico’s BMV Group halted trading at the country’s main exchange before the start of the Monday session.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Burnham’s big challenges on devolution
top_signals.summary: A boost for town centres and cities against the suburbs could come back to bite him

top_signals.title: NYSE Owner Plans Futures on Monetary Policy
top_signals.summary: Intercontinental Exchange Inc. is adding new futures contracts tied to global monetary-policy decisions and US natural gas storage. Sridhar Natarajan has more on "Bloomberg Open Interest." (Source: Bloomberg)

top_signals.title: Orbán hid half of Hungary’s budget deficit, new PM says
top_signals.summary: Péter Magyar says his predecessor ‘lied’ about 2026 shortfall of 8% of GDP
